passed_criteria: @Inject and @RestClient both present on the PetclinicService field in PetclinicMcpServer
No throws declared on any @Tool method
No new ObjectMapper() instantiation — ObjectMapper is injected via @Inject
At least one meaningful MCP test — listPetTypes_returnsSuccessResponse and getPet error handling test present
Sidecar container petclinic-mcp in Kubernetes manifest exposes containerPort 8888
Each @Tool description mentions its parameters and return value
All four DTOs (PetType, Visit, Pet, Owner) created in org.acme.dto package
PetclinicService interface name ends with 'Service' per Rule 02
summary: All 9 planned files generated correctly. 7 GET endpoints implemented in PetclinicService and wrapped as MCP tools in PetclinicMcpServer. application.properties configured with port 8888, CORS, and REST client URL. Kubernetes manifest updated with petclinic-mcp sidecar. pom.xml unchanged — all required dependencies were already present.
